pattern: A one-dimensional count of ways to reach each length, where every state extends by appending a block of zeros or a block of ones, sums the reachable lengths in the valid window.
hints: Only the current string length matters, since each step appends a fixed number of identical characters.
The number of ways to reach a length is the sum of the ways to reach the two shorter lengths you could have come from.
Fill an array f where f[i] counts strings of length i, with f[0] = 1, then add f[i] to the answer for every i between low and high, all modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: different block sizes over a wider window
block sizes larger than the lower bound skip short lengths
only one block size ever fits the window
equal block sizes make the recurrence double

stress.description: large window with small block sizes so many strings are counted
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    high = max(2, size)
    low = rng.randint(1, high)
    zero = rng.randint(1, 4)
    one = rng.randint(1, 4)
    return [low, high, zero, one]
stress.note: small block sizes keep the dp dense and the count large, exercising the modulo
